text: The surgeon uses a Keith needle with number one nylon to retract the dissected mesocolon, employing a novel technique to manage the tissue.

The surgeon threads a needle through the anterior abdominal wall, keeping the thread lax, and uses a Hemolock applicator to retract the mesocolon. The Hemolock is applied to the stitch and mesocolon, with a clip upgrade visible on the peritoneum. The needle is then brought back through the anterior abdominal wall in a retrograde fashion.
The surgeon identifies an optimal location on the left side for mesocolon retraction, ensuring it is securely retracted in a different area using the previously described technique.
The surgeon uses a Hemolock device to securely hold and retract the mesocolon, preventing potential tearing that can occur with thread-only techniques.
The surgeon uses a straight needle to retract the peritoneum and mesocolon tissue in a retrograde fashion, demonstrating effective tissue manipulation for surgical exposure.

The surgeon demonstrates effective retraction of the mesocolon using a Keith needle and Hemolock, visibly lifting the tissue away from the surgical field. This technique is shown to provide clear exposure without concern for major vascular structures.
